context: Everything you've built so far has been about you - "yo". But conversation is about other people too: where she works, what he does. The verb changes a little for each person, and once you see the pattern it's easy.
explanation: Every Spanish verb has a different ending for each person. You already use the "I" endings - hablo, trabajo, vivo, all ending in -o. First meet the people: yo (I), tu (you), el (he). Spanish so often drops these little words - because the ending already shows who - that "hablo" on its own means "I speak". Next you'll change those endings for other people.

examples.note: vivir is an -ir verb, so he/she ends in -e: vive.
